other_observers: Brandon Percival, David Chartier, Van Truan, Dave Silverman, Margie Joy, Nick Moore, and others; Brandon Percival with field trip from Audubon Society
specific_location: Pueblo Reservoir from the group picnick ground and farther north observation point near the South Shore Marina.
description: Jaeger by coloration and shape on the water and while flying, i.e. while chasing gulls.  In flight almost raptor like with, at times, powerful wing beats, covering ground very rapidly in comparison to nearby gulls. At rest, rufous buffy head and hind neck contrasted with darker brown body. When closest, bill longish and thin with no gonydeal angle, pale with darker tip to the tip of the bill. In flight the tail was short but with two pointed central tail rects, when together giving the impression of being blunt, however they were tapered to a point. Some undertail and breast barring slightly visible at times. Underwing, single white flash present. No darker bar of underwing coverts dividing the underwing white into two parts as in POJA. Bird looked identical to the Chatfiled bird I had seen closer 7 days earlier.Behaviors: spent much tine loafing far out in the water. Did make two purposeful chases of gulls with acrobatic swoops and dives. At one time flew straight from loafing position towards the dam in a fast, direct flight putting up two gulls but gulls apparently did not have any food in their crops as the jaeger gave up almost immediately.Call: none heardPlumage: immature
